source_file: The corporate social credit system in China and its transnational impact.txt
text:
4. Conclusion
This analysis has shown that the CSCS is border transcending in many ways.
Its characteristics amplify the intertwinement of legal categories and empha-
sise the factor of the individual in corporate decision-making which makes
this national regulatory tool a transnational legal problem. It is a transna-
tional legal problem that exempliﬁes the political aspects of transnational
law and as such it is a subject of this paper. Currently, the transnational pol-
itical project is working towards corporate and capitalist interests; it is
described as creating a common sense within the global political economy.
125
    European Commission, ‘Joint Communication to the European Parliament, the European Council and
    the Council: EU-China—a Strategic Outlook’(12 March 2019), online: https://ec.europa.eu/
    commission/sites/beta-political/ﬁles/communication-eu-china-a-strategic-outlook.pdf.
126
    And Western economic interest in China and the pursuit of power was consolidated via Western cor-
    porations and by international law. See Krisch (n 5) 383–4, 402.
127
    Martin Hála and Jichang Lulu, ‘The CCP’s model of social control goes global’ (2018) Sinopsis, online:
    https://sinopsis.cz/en/the-ccps-model-of-social-control-goes-global/.
128
    See Hoﬀman (n 11) 7–8.
                                              TRANSNATIONAL LEGAL THEORY       313
